title: ArAudienceKnowledgeConstraint
description: A15-SE3 (aristotelian-sketch-04 / dialect-compilation-surface-sketch-01 DCS1 side 1). Hard constraint: the audience must possess the named knowledge `subject` by tau_s <= latest_tau_s. When `source_event_id` is set, asserts the knowledge is delivered at that specific event. The `subject` is a free-text canonical knowledge claim; the dialect does not constrain its vocabulary (DOQ-AR4-2 banks the cross-encoding consistency question). Distinct from sketch-03 OQ-AP9 (audience-level recognition as a dialect record): A15-SE3 is an authorial constraint on knowledge timing, not a dialect-level recognition record. The two are architecturally orthogonal. A7.14 verifies structural integrity (subject non-empty after strip; latest_tau_s >= 0; source event resolves with tau_s <= latest_tau_s when substrate threaded). Whether the substrate's audience-projection state actually carries the knowledge is out of scope for Tier-2 (DOQ-AR4-1; pressures compilation-sketch-01 OQ1). Records live on ArMythos.audience_knowledge_constraints. PFS-N5..N8.

id.description: Stable identifier for this constraint (e.g., 'ak_polonius_behind_arras'). PFS-N5.

subject.description: Free-text canonical knowledge claim (e.g., 'claudius_killed_king_hamlet'). The dialect imposes no controlled vocabulary today; conventions are encoding-local. DOQ-AR4-2 banks the cross-encoding vocabulary discipline question. A7.14 check 1 separately enforces non-empty after strip (catches whitespace-only authoring that the schema's minLength=1 admits). PFS-N6.

latest_τ_s.description: Substrate τ_s by which the audience must possess the knowledge. minimum 0 (A7.14 check 2 is the second line of defense for negative Python values that bypass the schema). The compiler at stage 1 extracts to audience-state invariants; stage 3 plans substrate ensuring the knowledge is conveyed by this τ_s. PFS-N7.

source_event_id.description: Optional substrate event id at which the knowledge is delivered. When set, A7.14 check 3 enforces the event resolves in substrate (when threaded) and check 4 enforces the event's τ_s <= latest_τ_s. When absent, the constraint is purely a deadline — the compiler is free to deliver the knowledge at any qualifying substrate event up to and including latest_τ_s. Plain-string reference per PFS6-X2. PFS-N8.
